root_text.an4.170:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ānando kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme.
root_text.an4.170:1.3: Tatra kho āyasmā ānando bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an4.170:1.5: “Āvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato ānandassa paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an4.170:2.1: “Yo hi koci, āvuso, bhikkhu vā bhikkhunī vā mama santike arahattappattiṁ byākaroti, sabbo so catūhi maggehi, etesaṁ vā aññatarena.
root_text.an4.170:3.2: Idha, āvuso, bhikkhu samathapubbaṅgamaṁ vipassanaṁ bhāveti.
root_text.an4.170:3.3: Tassa samathapubbaṅgamaṁ vipassanaṁ bhāvayato maggo sañjāyati.
root_text.an4.170:3.4: So taṁ maggaṁ āsevati bhāveti bahulīkaroti.
root_text.an4.170:3.5: Tassa taṁ maggaṁ āsevato bhāvayato bahulīkaroto saṁyojanāni pahīyanti, anusayā byantīhonti.
root_text.an4.170:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu vipassanāpubbaṅgamaṁ samathaṁ bhāveti.
root_text.an4.170:4.2: Tassa vipassanāpubbaṅgamaṁ samathaṁ bhāvayato maggo sañjāyati.
root_text.an4.170:4.3: So taṁ maggaṁ āsevati bhāveti bahulīkaroti.
root_text.an4.170:4.4: Tassa taṁ maggaṁ āsevato bhāvayato bahulīkaroto saṁyojanāni pahīyanti, anusayā byantīhonti.
root_text.an4.170:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu samathavipassanaṁ yuganaddhaṁ bhāveti.
root_text.an4.170:5.2: Tassa samathavipassanaṁ yuganaddhaṁ bhāvayato maggo sañjāyati.
root_text.an4.170:5.3: So taṁ maggaṁ āsevati bhāveti bahulīkaroti.
root_text.an4.170:5.4: Tassa taṁ maggaṁ āsevato bhāvayato bahulīkaroto saṁyojanāni pahīyanti, anusayā byantīhonti.
root_text.an4.170:6.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhuno dhammuddhaccaviggahitaṁ mānasaṁ hoti.
root_text.an4.170:6.2: Hoti so, āvuso, samayo yaṁ taṁ cittaṁ ajjhattameva santiṭṭhati sannisīdati ekodi hoti samādhiyati.
root_text.an4.170:6.4: So taṁ maggaṁ āsevati bhāveti bahulīkaroti.
root_text.an4.170:6.5: Tassa taṁ maggaṁ āsevato bhāvayato bahulīkaroto saṁyojanāni pahīyanti, anusayā byantīhonti.
root_text.an4.170:7.1: Yo hi koci, āvuso, bhikkhu vā bhikkhunī vā mama santike arahattappattiṁ byākaroti, sabbo so imehi catūhi maggehi, etesaṁ vā aññatarenā”ti.

translation_text.an4.170:1.2: At one time Venerable Ānanda was staying near Kosambī, in Ghosita’s Monastery.
translation_text.an4.170:2.1: “Reverends, all of the monks and nuns who declare in my presence that they have attained perfection, did so by one or other of four paths.
translation_text.an4.170:3.2: Take a mendicant who develops serenity before discernment.
translation_text.an4.170:3.3: As they do so, the path is born in them.
translation_text.an4.170:3.4: They cultivate, develop, and make much of it.
translation_text.an4.170:3.5: By doing so, they give up the fetters and eliminate the underlying tendencies.
translation_text.an4.170:4.1: Another mendicant develops discernment before serenity.
translation_text.an4.170:4.2: As they do so, the path is born in them.
translation_text.an4.170:4.3: They cultivate, develop, and make much of it.
translation_text.an4.170:4.4: By doing so, they give up the fetters and eliminate the underlying tendencies.
translation_text.an4.170:5.1: Another mendicant develops serenity and discernment in conjunction.
translation_text.an4.170:5.2: As they do so, the path is born in them.
translation_text.an4.170:5.3: They cultivate, develop, and make much of it.
translation_text.an4.170:5.4: By doing so, they give up the fetters and eliminate the underlying tendencies.
translation_text.an4.170:6.1: Another mendicant’s heart is seized by restlessness to realize the teaching.
translation_text.an4.170:6.2: But there comes a time when their mind is stilled internally; it settles, unifies, and becomes immersed in samādhi.
translation_text.an4.170:6.3: The path is born in them.
translation_text.an4.170:6.4: They cultivate, develop, and make much of it.
translation_text.an4.170:6.5: By doing so, they give up the fetters and eliminate the underlying tendencies.
translation_text.an4.170:7.1: All of the monks and nuns who declare in my presence that they have attained perfection, did so by one or other of these four paths.”
